publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 23-24. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: We have just arrived; after a pleasant drive. But it has been a very warm day, altho' a good deal of wind too. Last night there was rain & it continued until 10 o'clock this morning.--
I was delighted & extremely interested by my visit to Bracket this morning.

I took care too to observe & enquire about all the things that I know you would have cared most about, had you been with me. I find Lord Melbourne plants a good deal; larch, fir, & oak & ash; but very much I believe the two former. It is a very beautiful place altogether. When I return, I shall tell you all about it.
No more now; but you see you are remembered
by the Bird, in all she sees.
I thought you would like to have even this shabby line tonight.--
The Hen is tolerable, but requires more bleeding; & I only fear she will not have decision to take the right measures.--
I hear a very sweet & beautiful Canary singing, chirrupping, whistling, & hopping, in his cage; poor little fellow!-- Continue
to direct always to St James' Sqre.
